description: A statistics/messaging card with a large black-and-white photographic portrait of a Black woman speaking into two microphones at a lectern; she wears a dark square-neck dress and a multi-strand pearl necklace (contextually New York Attorney General Letitia James, the target of the accompanying tweet). On the left, a semi-transparent navy panel holds the headline in a huge gold serif numeral '2,700' over white serif text 'criminal alien arrests in New York State.' The bottom is a solid navy band bearing the circular DHS seal and the white text 'U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement.' The monochrome treatment of the official, combined with the gold statistic, creates a stark political-attack aesthetic. Propaganda/messaging technique: pairs an enforcement statistic with the image of a Democratic state official the tweet accuses of wanting to 'let them roam free,' framing a political opponent as soft on crime and protective of 'criminal aliens.' Target audience: the general public and political base, to attack sanctuary-aligned officials.
notable_text: 2,700 criminal alien arrests in New York State; U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement
